Petitioner And Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Void Judgment And To Enter New Judgment Enforcing Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lynne Searle for (conservator) William Parisi. Ronald Mazzaferro (via court call) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Searle stated that there are 2 major issues, that the judgment is not sufficiently certain, that the amount due is not ascertainable without reference to information that is not in the judgment and that the prevailing party is entitled to costs and fees which are not ascertainable in the judgment. The Court stated that the judgment is clear and that it is the parties settlement. Ms. Searle stated that they are entitled to judgment that reflects total breach of contract, that the judgment needs to be voided and that they need a new judgment with amounts and referred to documents filed by Mr. Mazzaferro and Great Sunset. The Court responded that the judgment names amounts exactly as the settlement agreement stated. Ms. Searle stated that the judgment does not specify the prevailing party or who the amounts are owed to. Mr. Mazzaferro stated that the documents Ms. Searle referred to are documents that were filed prior to the hearing resulting in the judgment and underlying order and that he found no flaw in the tentative ruling. The Court stated that the tentative ruling is confirmed, that this does not mean that Mr. Mazzaferro has not been in breach of obligations, that it does not mean there are not other steps that can be taken, referred to 664.6, that there are not always prevailing parties under a settlement, that this motion makes no sense and is denied and that Mr. Mazzaferro is to submit a form of order consistent with the tentative ruling.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Court Reporter: Barrie Hart, CSR #6954, 415-328-6644 (Reported)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Appointment Of Receiver
Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lynn Searle, Esq. (415-986-7500 ext107) for Petitioner William Parisi.
Russell Stanaland, Esq.(415-781-4000) for Plaintiff/Moving party Edith Mazzaferri.
Ronald Mazzaferro (707-703-0599) for Defendant/opposing party in Pro Per.

Plaintiff submits to the tentative ruling. Defendant argues that he has a peremptory challenge to Judge Quidachay and that he needs a Court reporter for the proceedings so an appeal can be filed. Defendant does not waive his right to a Court Reporter. Defendant argues the matter has been fully litigated and denied by Judge Busch. Defendant cites paragraphs in his Opposition. Plaintiff argues Judge Quidachay and Judge Busch did not rule on the merits of the case. The Court looks at the July 8, 2015 order of Judge Wong. Plaintiff argues the need for a receiver and that Dept. 501 is the appropriate Department. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Appointment of Receiver is Granted. Plaintiff submits a proposed order. Receiver to post bond for $100,000*. Appointed receiver to come back this afternoon at 1:30 pm in Dept. 502 to take an oath. The Court emails a copy of the Order to Defendant at racinmazzie@aol.com. Parties ordered back at 2pm in Dept. 502 for the Order to be signed. Plaintiff to bring receiver for oath and posting of bond. Judge: A. James Robertson, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

*After the hearing in Dept. 501, an Order is signed in Dept. 502. Receiver to post bond for $50,000.*

Order Granting Plaintiff, Edith Mazzaferri'S Appointment Of Receiver
Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Lynn Searle, Esq. (415-986-7500 ext107) for Petitioner William Parisi.
Russell Stanaland, Esq.(415-781-4000) for Plaintiff/Moving party Edith Mazzaferri.
Ronald Mazzaferro (707-703-0599) for Defendant/opposing party in Pro Per. Courtcall
David A. Bradlow, Trustee-Receiver is sworn.

Defendant argues that he has a peremptory challenge to Judge Quidachay and now wants to challenge nunc pro tunc Judge A. James Robertson, II. Judge Robertson denies Defendant's challenge. The Court states that he must challenge a specific Judge and may not challenge the department. Defendant request an evidentiary hearing and requests a Court reporter to be present.

Counsel for plaintiff request a reduction in the amount of the bond from $100,000.to $50, 000. Plaintiff edith massaferri's motion for appointment of receiver is GRANTED and the Court grants a reduction in the bond and set is at $50,000. The Receiver, Mr. Bradlow informs the Court that once the order has been signed he wil be able to secure the bond and file it as soon as possible. Order dinged in open court. Counsel for plaintiff shall provide a copy of the order to defendant Ronald Mazzaferro. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: R. Goulding; Not Reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar May-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nilesh Choudhary for the Corporations. Ronald Mazzaferro in Propria Persona via court call.

Mr. Chaoudhary stated that he has no further argument. Mr. Mazzaferro stated that on 11/25/14 he filed a notice of stay of proceedings, that a fee arbitration is taking place in Placer County and cited Business & Professions code 6201, that on 3/5/15 he filed a further declaration that the arbitration is ongoing, that if this proceeding goes forward the fee arbitration will be waived and that he was not served with all documents. Mr. Choudhary stated that the request for a stay per 6201 only applies where the action pending is in regards to that fee, that it is not the situation here, that there is no basis for the stay and that there is another pending appeal and significant conflicts. The Court took the 2 motions to be relieved as counsel under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff Edith Mazzaferri'S Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Requests For Adminssions And Genuiness Of Documents; Motion To Continue Discovery
Off Calendar Aug-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Russell Stanaland and Annie Willis for Edith Mazzaferri (Moving Party). Ronald Mazzaferro (Respondent) appeared by Court Call. James Chandler appeared by Court Call for Great Sunset Ventures (Respondent).

Mr. Mazzaferro stated that he has no problem with the tentative ruling except for granting of the sanctions in the amount of $3,500.00 against him. Mr. Mazzaferro and Mr. Stanaland presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to sanctions. After hearing the arguments, the Court revised the ruling on sanctions and adopted the rest of the tentative ruling as follow: Edith Mazzaferri's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents and Requests for Admission and Genuineness of Documents directed to Ronald Mazzaferro is granted. Ronald Mazzaferro must serve further responses no later than September 13, 2013. To the extent Ronald Mazzaferro claims a privilege against self-incrimination, he must make a specific showing that each discovery question or request might tend to incriminate him. To the extent Ronald Mazzaferro asserts the attorney client privilege he must provide sufficient factual information for the propounding party to evaluate the merits of the claim, including a privilege log. The Motion to Continue is off calendar as moot. Mr. Stanaland to prepare the order as revised.

Mr. Chandler argued that he has no personal knowledge to verify the responses and that Mr. Mazzaferro is only person that can verify the responses. The Court stated that it is not before the court today and that there is no request for oral argument. The tentative ruling is adopted in entirety. Edith Mazzaferri's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Admission and Genuineness of Documents directed to Great Sunset Ventures (GSV) is granted. No opposition filed. GSV must provide further responses by September 13, 2013. To the extent GSV asserts the attorney client privilege, it must provide sufficient factual information for the propounding party to evaluate the merits of the cliam, including a privilege log. The Motion to Continue is off calendar as moot. The Motion for Sanctions is granted. GSV shall pay moving party sanctions of $2,000 within 20 days. Mr. Stanaland to prepare the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Sr. Deputy M. Benton. Not Reported.
